justifications.completeness: Detects only one of four pedestrians and two of five vehicles, missing the majority of objects in the scene.

justifications.spatial_relations: Includes some plausible relations such as behind the truck and next to the sidewalk but omits spatial relations for most objects and is imprecise for the pedestrian placement.
justifications.overall: Partial and partially accurate extraction with correct labels for a few entities but overall sparse detections and incomplete spatial and object coverage.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one of four pedestrians and one of six vehicles, so many objects are missing.

justifications.spatial_relations: Includes plausible relations (pedestrian left of truck, truck next to building) but omits relations for other actors and cannot be fully validated against the provided ground truth.
justifications.overall: Accurate attribute labeling for the few detected entities is counterbalanced by very low recall of pedestrians and vehicles, limiting overall usefulness.

justifications.completeness: Missing one of two pedestrians and three of four vehicles, so many scene elements are omitted.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extracted several attributes (age, actions, trunk/door states) adding detail but also includes unsupported or potentially incorrect attributes and omits many object instances.

justifications.overall: Extraction captures some correct semantics but omits numerous objects and includes uncertain attributes, yielding a partial and inconsistent scene understanding.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one of two pedestrians and 2 of 5 vehicles, missing several objects and therefore not covering the scene contents.

justifications.overall: Partial semantic detail is outweighed by poor completeness and some inconsistent labels, yielding a moderate to low overall extraction quality.

justifications.completeness: Missed all three pedestrians and detected only 2 of 5 vehicles, so coverage of scene objects is very low.

justifications.overall: Overall the extraction captures a few correct vehicle attributes but is significantly incomplete and misses key pedestrian detections and broader scene context.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 1 of 3 pedestrians and 2 of 6 vehicles, missing the majority of objects and their identities.
justifications.semantic_richness: Includes useful attributes (actions, context, vehicle types, door/trunk states) for reported objects but coverage is limited and lacks track IDs linking to ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides reasonable local relations for the reported objects (sidewalk, on the road, next to) but omits relations for most scene objects and some relations are imprecise.
justifications.overall: Good attribute detail for a small subset of detections but poor recall of pedestrians and vehicles reduces overall usefulness.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one of four pedestrians and three of six vehicles, missing the majority of objects.
justifications.semantic_richness: Per-object attributes such as actions, context, and door/trunk states are provided, but many detections lack fuller annotations and one ground-truth transporting attribute was missed.
justifications.spatial_relations: A few plausible pairwise relations are reported, but relations are sparse and cannot be fully validated against the provided ground truth.
justifications.overall: Reasonable attribute detail for detected items is outweighed by poor object coverage and incomplete mapping to ground-truth entities.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only one of six pedestrians and two of seven vehicles from the ground truth, so most entities are missing.

justifications.spatial_relations: Some plausible spatial relations are provided (behind/next to/near shop) but they are sparse and not comprehensive relative to the scene, and the ground truth does not explicitly confirm them.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one of many pedestrians and one of several vehicles, missing the majority of ground-truth objects.

justifications.spatial_relations: Includes a couple of spatial relations but they are sparse, imprecise, and not well aligned with specific ground-truth relations such as jaywalking or walking along the side.
justifications.overall: Sparse detections with somewhat accurate attributes for the few identified entities yield low overall utility compared to the comprehensive ground truth.

justifications.completeness: Many ground-truth actors and vehicles are missing and several motion and context attributes are not reported.

justifications.spatial_relations: Provided spatial relations are vague and partly inconsistent with ground-truth contexts, so spatial detail is limited.
justifications.overall: The extraction captures some useful attributes but omits most actors and contains several semantic and spatial mismatches, producing a low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Extraction covers only one pedestrian and two vehicles versus seven pedestrians and five vehicles in the ground truth, missing the majority of objects.

justifications.completeness: Only 2 pedestrians were detected versus 7 in the ground truth and one vehicle is missing, so many objects and attributes are omitted.

justifications.completeness: Captures 4 of 11 annotated objects (2 of 7 persons and 2 of 4 vehicles), missing most pedestrians and parked vehicles.

justifications.completeness: Many objects are missing: only 2 of 6 ground-truth pedestrians and 2 of 5 vehicles were reported, so overall coverage is poor.

justifications.completeness: Only 2 of 5 pedestrians and 1 of 5 vehicles are reported, missing multiple actors and many ground truth attributes.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 2 of 5 ground-truth pedestrians and 2 of 5 vehicles and missed several communicative/contextual annotations, so coverage is low.

justifications.completeness: The extraction captures only a subset of pedestrians and vehicles from the ground truth and omits multiple objects and several vehicle instances.

justifications.completeness: Many objects are missing: the VLM reports 2 pedestrians versus 4 in GT and 2 vehicles versus 5, so coverage is poor.

justifications.overall: Overall the output is partially accurate but notably incomplete with limited semantic depth and inconsistent spatial information.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 2 of 4 pedestrians and 2 of 6 vehicles, missing multiple objects and therefore substantially incomplete.

justifications.completeness: The extraction detected only 2 of 6 pedestrians and 2 of 4 vehicles, missing several pedestrians and parked/stopped vehicles.

justifications.completeness: Detected 2 of 4 pedestrians and 1 of 4 vehicles, missing several objects and thus providing incomplete coverage of the scene.

justifications.completeness: Detected two of four pedestrians and one of three annotated vehicles while introducing an extra bicycle, so many objects are missing or incorrect.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 2 of 5 pedestrians and 2 of 3 vehicles from the ground truth, so coverage of objects in the scene is low.

justifications.overall: Good coverage of entities and actions with useful extra context, but the incorrect age label reduces reliability and lowers overall score.

justifications.completeness: The extraction correctly detects the person, action (sitting), and absence of vehicles but misses the ground-truth age label and an explicit track id, reducing completeness.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output provides additional cues such as a track hint and spatial relation and matches several contextual attributes, though the age label is incorrect which lowers semantic fidelity.
justifications.spatial_relations: A spatial relation is provided (next to the wall) but the ground truth contains no spatial relation to verify it, so it is neither confirmed nor refuted.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is largely correct in detecting the person and action and adds useful context, but the incorrect age label and missing track id limit its reliability.

justifications.completeness: Most relevant attributes are present and the action matches ground truth, but the age is incorrect and an explicit track identifier is missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes action, clothing hint, and spatial relation but omits the fine-grained age label and an explicit object track id, reducing detail.
justifications.spatial_relations: The description correctly states the person is sitting on the side of the road, which matches the scene context.
justifications.overall: Accurate action and spatial information give strong utility, but the incorrect age classification and missing track id lower overall reliability.
